headline: AI trade could power higher in H2 2026
detail: The piece suggests AI momentum may not be done, with relatively low bar for upside surprises. That directly supports your largest exposures: CRWD, SMH, SOXL, QQQ, and QQQI all move with AI/tech sentiment. A real AI spending catalyst here could be the engine for the summer rally Wells Fargo is also calling.
rec: No action yet - this is a thesis, not a confirmed event. If AI capex news or earnings beats materialize, CRWD (scanner entry 741-742, stop 740) is your cleanest single-name expression. Watch before adding.

headline: Wells Fargo calls for summer rally
detail: A major bank telling clients to get long into summer is a sentiment tailwind for SPY, QQQ, and your tech-heavy book. It reinforces holding your index positions (SPY, QQQ) through their current stops rather than trimming early.
rec: No action needed - you're already long SPY and QQQ with healthy stops at 705 and 670. Let the thesis play out; tighten stops only if either breaks below recent support.

headline: 20 worst-performing S&P 500 stocks in H1 2026
detail: This is a reminder that even in a bull market, concentrated losers exist. Your SOXL at -7% and SMH at -0.69% put chips on the underperformer watchlist. Leverage decay in SOXL means it can underperform even when the underlying index recovers.
rec: No trade, but check whether any chip names on that 'worst performers' list are core SOXL holdings - if chip fundamentals are the drag, not just volatility, the $200 stop becomes more urgent, not less.

headline: Oil prices set for steep monthly drop
detail: Falling oil is mild positive for tech - lower energy costs reduce data center operating expenses and ease inflation pressure, which is good for rate-sensitive growth names like your entire book. Not a direct catalyst but a background tailwind.
rec: No trade warranted from this alone. It's a soft positive for macro but does not give a specific entry or exit signal for any of your holdings.
